TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}
TEXT.source: told by Giɫgiɫaw of Vaɫulu village

S.FORM: nu misepi tjen tua tsemdatsedas tua maɫiaɫia, nanguaq aravats.
S.PHON: nu misəpi cən tua ʦəmdaʦədas tua maʎiaʎia, naŋuaq aravaʦ.
S.FORM: nu misepi tjen tua cemdacedas tua maljialjia, nanguaq aravac.
S.PHON: nu misəpi ʦən tua ʦəmdaʦədas tua maʎiaʎia, naŋuaq aravaʦ.
S.TRANSL: When we dream of the rising sun or dawn it is very good.

S.FORM: matu tsemdatsedas a tja varung; tja sipuqimaqimang tua nemanemanga.
S.PHON: matu ʦəmdaʦədas a ca varuŋ; ca sipuqimaqimaŋ tua nəmanəmaŋa.
S.FORM: matu cemdacedas a tja varung; tja sipuqimaqimang tua nemanemanga.
S.PHON: matu ʦəmdaʦədas a ʦa varuŋ; ʦa sipuqimaqimaŋ tua nəmanəmaŋa.
S.TRANSL: It is as if our hearts were rising. We will have good luck in everything.

S.FORM: nu ɫemakaɫakay itjen, uri kisudjuan itjen.
S.PHON: nu ʎəmakaʎakaj icən, uri kisuɉuan icən.
S.FORM: nu ljemakaljakay itjen, uri kisudjuan itjen.
S.PHON: nu ʎəmakaʎakaj iʦən, uri kisuɉuan iʦən.
S.TRANSL: If women dream they are on a swing, someone is in love with them. (Note: Swinging of the bride is part of the wedding ceremony.)

S.FORM: nu i vavaw itjen tua kavayan, uri putsekeɫ itjen tua mamazangiɫan.
S.PHON: nu i vavaw icən tua kavajan, uri puʦəkəʎ icən tua mamazaŋiʎan.
S.FORM: nu i vavaw itjen tua kavayan, uri pucekelj itjen tua mamazangiljan.
S.PHON: nu i vavaw iʦən tua kavajan, uri puʦəkəʎ iʦən tua mamazaŋiʎan.
S.TRANSL: If we are up a bambusa tree, we will marry a chieftain.

S.FORM: nu misepi tjen tua zangaw, uri pupaisu itjen.
S.PHON: nu misəpi cən tua zaŋaw, uri pupaisu icən.
S.FORM: nu misepi tjen tua zangaw, uri pupaisu itjen.
S.PHON: nu misəpi ʦən tua zaŋaw, uri pupaisu iʦən.
S.TRANSL: When we dream of the leaf for wrapping betel nut, we will be rich.

S.FORM: nu patsun itjen tua kamaya a malulum, uri matu lum a kinakesan nua tja vaqu.
S.PHON: nu paʦun icən tua kamaja a maɭuɭum, uri matu ɭum a kinakəsan nua ca vaqu.
S.FORM: nu pacun itjen tua kamaya a malulum, uri matu lum a kinakesan nua tja vaqu.
S.PHON: nu paʦun iʦən tua kamaja a maɭuɭum, uri matu ɭum a kinakəsan nua ʦa vaqu.
S.TRANSL: When we see ripe mangoes, our millet will be fully ripened.

S.FORM: nu misepi tjen tua tsiqaw, namaya uta sipuvaquvaqu.
S.PHON: nu misəpi cən tua ʦiqaw, namaja uta sipuvaquvaqu.
S.FORM: nu misepi tjen tua ciqaw, namaya uta sipuvaquvaqu.
S.PHON: nu misəpi ʦən tua ʦiqaw, namaja uta sipuvaquvaqu.
S.TRANSL: When we dream about fish, this is also about our millet crop.

S.FORM: nu kakeḍikeḍian a tsiqaw, uri kakeḍikeḍian uta a tja vaqu.
S.PHON: nu kakəɖikəɖian a ʦiqaw, uri kakəɖikəɖian uta a ca vaqu.
S.FORM: nu kakedrikedrian a ciqaw, uri kakedrikedrian uta a tja vaqu.
S.PHON: nu kakəɖikəɖian a ʦiqaw, uri kakəɖikəɖian uta a ʦa vaqu.
S.TRANSL: If the fish are small ones, our millet also will have small grains.

S.FORM: nu itua ɫimeɫimek a tsiqaw, uri putsemel aravats a tja puvaquan.
S.PHON: nu itua ʎiməʎimək a ʦiqaw, uri puʦəməɭ aravaʦ a ca puvaquan.
S.FORM: nu itua ljimeljimek a ciqaw, uri pucemel aravac a tja puvaquan.
S.PHON: nu itua ʎiməʎimək a ʦiqaw, uri puʦəməɭ aravaʦ a ʦa puvaquan.
S.TRANSL: If the fish are in muddy water, there will be a lot of weeds in our millet fields.

S.FORM: nu matsatsay, nanguaq aravats, sika merakarakats.
S.PHON: nu maʦaʦaj, naŋuaq aravaʦ, sika mərakarakaʦ.
S.FORM: nu macacay, nanguaq aravac, sika merakarakac.
S.PHON: nu maʦaʦaj, naŋuaq aravaʦ, sika mərakarakaʦ.
S.TRANSL: If there is death or a funeral it is very good; the person seen will be strong.

S.FORM: nu sevatsavatsaq a zaɫum i tjumaq, sipulamilami aya.
S.PHON: nu səvaʦavaʦaq a zaʎum i cumaq, sipuɭamiɭami aja.
S.FORM: nu sevacavacaq a zaljum i tjumaq, sipulamilami aya.
S.PHON: nu səvaʦavaʦaq a zaʎum i ʦumaq, sipuɭamiɭami aja.
S.TRANSL: If water is coming out indoors, it means there will be plenty of staple foods.

S.FORM: nu misepi tjen tua qatsang tua qatjuvi tua nemanemanga a kakeḍikeḍian, tja aɫak a penuɫat.
S.PHON: nu misəpi cən tua qaʦaŋ tua qacuvi tua nəmanəmaŋa a kakəɖikəɖian, ca aʎak a pənuʎat.
S.FORM: nu misepi tjen tua qacang tua qatjuvi tua nemanemanga a kakedrikedrian, tja aljak a penuljat.
S.PHON: nu misəpi ʦən tua qaʦaŋ tua qaʦuvi tua nəmanəmaŋa a kakəɖikəɖian, ʦa aʎak a pənuʎat.
S.TRANSL: If we dream about small pigs or snakes or anything, it’s all to do with our children.

S.FORM: ɫakua nu kakuḍakuḍalan, nakuya tsemas.
S.PHON: ʎakua nu kakuɖakuɖaɭan, nakuja ʦəmas.
S.FORM: ljakua nu kakudrakudralan, nakuya cemas.
S.PHON: ʎakua nu kakuɖakuɖaɭan, nakuja ʦəmas.
S.TRANSL: But if they are big ones, it refers to evil spirits.

S.FORM: nu tsemuɫutsuɫu itjen tua qatsang tua qemuzimuzip, uri izua matsay tjanuitjen a tatsekeɫan.
S.PHON: nu ʦəmuʎuʦuʎu icən tua qaʦaŋ tua qəmuzimuzip, uri izua maʦaj canuicən a taʦəkəʎan.
S.FORM: nu cemuljuculju itjen tua qacang tua qemuzimuzip, uri izua macay tjanuitjen a tacekeljan.
S.PHON: nu ʦəmuʎuʦuʎu iʦən tua qaʦaŋ tua qəmuzimuzip, uri izua maʦaj ʦanuiʦən a taʦəkəʎan.
S.TRANSL: If we are butchering a pig or other animal, there will be a death in our family.

S.FORM: nu maqevuqevutj a sapuy a tja djinadjas, uri kemalavut itjen tua nasi nua tsautsau.
S.PHON: nu maqəvuqəvuc a sapuj a ca ɉinaɉas, uri kəmaɭavut icən tua nasi nua ʦauʦau.
S.FORM: nu maqevuqevutj a sapuy a tja djinadjas, uri kemalavut itjen tua nasi nua caucau.
S.PHON: nu maqəvuqəvuʦ a sapuj a ʦa ɉinaɉas, uri kəmaɭavut iʦən tua nasi nua ʦauʦau.
S.TRANSL: If a flame we are holding is going out, we will be responsible for the death of someone(?).

S.FORM: nu patsutsun itjen sakamaya, uri seɫavak itjen tua masunasinasi.
S.PHON: nu paʦuʦun icən sakamaja, uri səʎavak icən tua masunasinasi.
S.FORM: nu pacucun itjen sakamaya, uri seljavak itjen tua masunasinasi.
S.PHON: nu paʦuʦun iʦən sakamaja, uri səʎavak iʦən tua masunasinasi.
S.TRANSL: If we just see this, we will be present at a death.

S.FORM: nu qezemezemetj nu masulesulem, uri matu qezemezemetj a tja varung tua tja kininemneman.
S.PHON: nu qəzəməzəməc nu masuɭəsuɭəm, uri matu qəzəməzəməc a ca varuŋ tua ca kininəmnəman.
S.FORM: nu qezemezemetj nu masulesulem, uri matu qezemezemetj a tja varung tua tja kininemneman.
S.PHON: nu qəzəməzəməʦ nu masuɭəsuɭəm, uri matu qəzəməzəməʦ a ʦa varuŋ tua ʦa kininəmnəman.
S.TRANSL: If it is night or getting dark, the thoughts of our hearts will be like night.

S.FORM: nu izua vaqu i tjanuitjen, izua uri matsay.
S.PHON: nu izua vaqu i canuicən, izua uri maʦaj.
S.FORM: nu izua vaqu i tjanuitjen, izua uri macay.
S.PHON: nu izua vaqu i ʦanuiʦən, izua uri maʦaj.
S.TRANSL: If we have millet, there will be a death.

S.FORM: nu izua qemaluqalu tua tsinqadjan, uri izua matsay i djalan.
S.PHON: nu izua qəmaɭuqaɭu tua ʦinqaɉan, uri izua maʦaj i ɉaɭan.
S.FORM: nu izua qemaluqalu tua cinqadjan, uri izua macay i djalan.
S.PHON: nu izua qəmaɭuqaɭu tua ʦinqaɉan, uri izua maʦaj i ɉaɭan.
S.TRANSL: If someone is carrying cut planks of wood, someone will die on a track.

S.FORM: nu misepi tjen tu kemakan itjen tua vutjuɫ tua qavay, sika saqetjuqetju aya.
S.PHON: nu misəpi cən tu kəmakan icən tua vucuʎ tua qavaj, sika saqəcuqəcu aja.
S.FORM: nu misepi tjen tu kemakan itjen tua vutjulj tua qavay, sika saqetjuqetju aya.
S.PHON: nu misəpi ʦən tu kəmakan iʦən tua vuʦuʎ tua qavaj, sika saqəʦuqəʦu aja.
S.TRANSL: If we dream that we are eating meat or millet dumplings, it is said to be a sign of illness.

S.FORM: nu keman itjen tua sutjaw tua ɫuaɫu tua paday, siqesaqesang.
S.PHON: nu kəman icən tua sucaw tua ʎuaʎu tua padaj, siqəsaqəsaŋ.
S.FORM: nu keman itjen tua sutjaw tua ljualju tua paday, siqesaqesang.
S.PHON: nu kəman iʦən tua suʦaw tua ʎuaʎu tua padaj, siqəsaqəsaŋ.
S.TRANSL: If we are eating peanuts, sweets or rice, it’s a sign of a cough.

S.FORM: nu seman umaumaq itjen, izua uri matsay a sisan luvang tjanuitjen.
S.PHON: nu səman umaumaq icən, izua uri maʦaj a sisan ɭuvaŋ canuicən.
S.FORM: nu seman umaumaq itjen, izua uri macay a sisan luvang tjanuitjen.
S.PHON: nu səman umaumaq iʦən, izua uri maʦaj a sisan ɭuvaŋ ʦanuiʦən.
S.TRANSL: If we are repairing a house, someone will die and we will have to dig a grave (or a grave will be dug for us).

S.FORM: nu mulaulaw itjen tua tja turivetsan, sipatsatsay nua tja aɫak.
S.PHON: nu muɭauɭaw icən tua ca turivəʦan, sipaʦaʦaj nua ca aʎak.
S.FORM: nu mulaulaw itjen tua tja turivecan, sipacacay nua tja aljak.
S.PHON: nu muɭauɭaw iʦən tua ʦa turivəʦan, sipaʦaʦaj nua ʦa aʎak.
S.TRANSL: If we have lost our tool, our child will die.

S.FORM: nu vaivaik itjen a sema tjuqaɫa, nakuya; sivaivaik nua tja avavak.
S.PHON: nu vaivaik icən a səma cuqaʎa, nakuja; sivaivaik nua ca avavak.
S.FORM: nu vaivaik itjen a sema tjuqalja, nakuya; sivaivaik nua tja avavak.
S.PHON: nu vaivaik iʦən a səma ʦuqaʎa, nakuja; sivaivaik nua ʦa avavak.
S.TRANSL: If we are going to another village, that is bad; our souls will depart.

S.FORM: nu venliveli tjen tua makaliɫaw, kapuɫu aya.
S.PHON: nu vənɭivəɭi cən tua makaɭiʎaw, kapuʎu aja.
S.FORM: nu venliveli tjen tua makaliljaw, kapulju aya.
S.PHON: nu vənɭivəɭi ʦən tua makaɭiʎaw, kapuʎu aja.
S.TRANSL: If we are buying cloth, it is a sign of mourning.

S.FORM: nu namitung itjen tua kaḍaḍi.
S.PHON: nu namituŋ icən tua kaɖaɖi.
S.FORM: nu namitung itjen tua kadradri.
S.PHON: nu namituŋ iʦən tua kaɖaɖi.
S.TRANSL: If we are wearing rags,

S.FORM: uri masan zepzepan itjen.
S.PHON: uri masan zəpzəpan icən.
S.FORM: uri masan zepzepan itjen.
S.PHON: uri masan zəpzəpan iʦən.
S.TRANSL: we will become infamous.

S.FORM: nu mavanavanaw itjen, sika saqetjuqetju aya.
S.PHON: nu mavanavanaw icən, sika saqəcuqəcu aja.
S.FORM: nu mavanavanaw itjen, sika saqetjuqetju aya.
S.PHON: nu mavanavanaw iʦən, sika saqəʦuqəʦu aja.
S.TRANSL: If we are bathing, it is said to be a sign of illness.
